_comment: XELIS Vault v5.0 — example custom_sources.json. Copy to ~/.xelis-vault/config/custom_sources.json and edit the REPLACE_ME placeholders. The file may be either a top-level array of sources OR an object with a 'sources' key (as shown here). API keys are read locally and NEVER logged.

sources._comment: EXAMPLE 1 — CoinEx (built-in type). Just provide api_key/api_secret if you have them; otherwise the public endpoint is used with stricter rate limits.
sources.name: coinex

sources._comment: EXAMPLE 2 — MEXC (built-in type). Same as CoinEx — set api_key/api_secret to raise rate limits.
sources.name: mexc

sources._comment: EXAMPLE 3 — XELIS native daemon. Reads the implied XEL/USD price from your local node. NOTE: the default implementation in price_provider.py returns None — implement the real pool-price derivation (XEL/xUSD pool on VaultSwap) by editing DaemonSource.fetch_price().
sources.name: xelis_daemon

sources._comment: EXAMPLE 4 — Generic HTTP source template. Fetches any JSON URL and walks the dot-separated json_path to find the price.
sources.name: custom_http

sources._comment: EXAMPLE 5 — Simple HTTP source (price at root).
sources.name: simple_api

sources._comment: EXAMPLE 6 — External command source. Runs a local script and parses stdout as JSON with a 'price' field. Useful for OTC desks, internal price aggregators, or hardware price feeds.
sources.name: my_script

sources._comment: EXAMPLE 7 — Python script source (variant of command).
sources.name: python_fetcher

_notes: PRIVACY: API keys in this file are NEVER logged. The provider script only logs source name + price + timestamp.
RECOMMENDED: Enable at least 2 sources so the median is robust. 3+ is better.
STALENESS: prices older than 30 seconds are rejected before computing the median.
OUTLIERS: prices more than 5% from the median are rejected before submitting to the oracle.
FEEDS: to add new feeds (XEL/BTC, XAU/USD, ...), create them on-chain via StakedOracle.add_feed (entry ID 0) and start a separate price_provider.py instance with --feed-id N.
